steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: The graph passes through $(-6,0)$, $(2,0)$, and $(5,0)$, so the zeros of $f$ are $x = -6$, $x = 2$, and $x = 5$.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: By the Factor Theorem: if $f(a) = 0$, then $(x - a)$ is a factor. So the factors corresponding to the zeros are $(x+6)$, $(x-2)$, and $(x-5)$.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content: Scan the choices for any of these three factors: $(x+6)$ is not listed; $(x-2)$ is the correct answer; $(x-5)$ is not listed.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.content: The matching choice, $(x-2)$, must be a factor because $f(2) = 0$.

choiceAnalysis:
• Choice A - $x+2$: This factor implies a zero at $x = -2$. The graph does not pass through $(-2, 0)$, so $(x+2)$ need not be a factor. ✗
  • Choice B - $x+5$: This factor implies a zero at $x = -5$. The graph does not pass through $(-5, 0)$, so $(x+5)$ need not be a factor. ✗
  • Choice C - $x-2$: This factor implies a zero at $x = 2$. The graph passes through $(2, 0)$, confirming $f(2) = 0$, so $(x-2)$ must be a factor by the Factor Theorem. ✓
  • Choice D - $x-6$: This factor implies a zero at $x = 6$. The graph does not pass through $(6, 0)$, so $(x-6)$ need not be a factor. ✗
C is correct.
